title: ericoides azoricum
intro: Ericoides azoricum, also known as Azor Eric, is a small shrub that belongs to the heath family and is native to the Azores Islands in Portugal. Its leaves and stems have been traditionally used in folk medicine due to their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health. In herbal preparations, Ericoides azoricum is often used as a decoction or infusion, and it can be combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
